    "pros": "The applicant clearly articulates a career trajectory from clinical practice to becoming an expert practitioner academic (g.1.a). Strong mentorship is evidenced by detailed supervisor profiles, protected time, and structured meeting frequencies (g.1.d). The research direction is distinctive, focusing on a specific gap in ventilator-assisted children's feeding disorders (g.1.b).",
    "drawbacks": "While the vision is clear, the 'distinctive ideas' (g.1.b) are an extension of existing work rather than a radical new paradigm, though this is appropriate for a doctoral fellowship. No material caveats prevent a high score."
  },

    ],
    "pros": "The applicant explicitly states why this award is the right next step, building on a completed PCAF to explore specific research gaps (g.2.a, g.2.b). The application details how the award will enable new skills (e.g., advanced mixed methods, leadership) and expand research partnerships with international centers and charities (g.2.c, g.2.d). The narrative connects the award directly to the applicant's career progression and the need to drive evidence-based initiatives.",
    "drawbacks": "No significant drawbacks; the justification is robust and directly evidenced."

    ],
    "pros": "The applicant demonstrates extensive prior research experience, including a scoping review and qualitative study on the exact topic of the proposed project (g.3.a). Formal training is well-documented, including an MRes, Designing Clinical Research course, and GCP training (g.3.b). There is a perfect alignment between prior experience (feeding/swallowing in ventilated children) and the skills required for the proposed study (g.3.c).",

    "pros": "The applicant lists multiple peer-reviewed publications (2024) and presentations at national/international forums (g.4.a). Awards include a Dean's List distinction and a Public Involvement Fund grant (g.4.c). The impact of previous work is described through dissemination to clinical networks and influencing service development (g.4.d).",
    "drawbacks": "Citation metrics (g.4.b) are not explicitly provided for the applicant's own work (though supervisor metrics are), which prevents a perfect 5, though the quality of outputs is otherwise strong."

Participation in leadership programs (Clinical Leadership course) is planned (g.5.c). Recognition is shown through invitations to present and network leadership roles (g.5.d).",
    "drawbacks": "While leadership potential is strong, the applicant is early in their academic career, so 'recognition by peers' (g.5.d) is emerging rather than established at a senior level, hence a 4."

      "sectdars__001_d",
      "sectdars__001_e",
      "sectdars__001_f"
    ],
    "pros": "The applicant explicitly states a commitment to a practitioner-academic career (g.6.a). The proposal includes 20% time for professional practice to maintain clinical competencies (g.6.c). Involvement in clinical research is extensive, with the applicant currently working as a respiratory lead (g.6.b). Participation in professional networks (e.g., Paediatric Dysphagia Speech and Language Therapists in Research) is well-documented (g.6.d).",
    "drawbacks": "No drawbacks; the commitment to the practitioner-academic model is explicit, well-resourced, and integrated into the project plan."
